questions.question: A passive sentence uses {a1}.
Active: People clean this room. Passive: This room {a1}.
In the passive sentence, the object becomes the {a1}.
Active: Maria wrote the report. Passive: The report was written {a1}.
Use a by-phrase when the {a1} is important.
The form of be changes according to the {a1}.
The passive needs the past participle: The window was {a1}.
Passive is useful when the doer is {a1}.
Correct the mistake: The letter written yesterday. The letter {a1} yesterday.
Active: They built the bridge. Passive: The bridge {a1}.
The passive structure places the focus on the {a1}.
Use with for an instrument: The note was written {a1}.
